# RBS Codebase — Coding Conventions

## Namespaces

- All code lives under `namespace rbs { ... }`.
- Sub-namespaces per RAT module: `rbs::lte`, `rbs::umts`, `rbs::gsm`.
- HAL and OMS stay in the root `rbs` namespace.
- Do NOT add `using namespace rbs` in headers.

## Naming

| Construct | Convention | Example |
|-----------|-----------|---------|
| Types / classes / enums | `PascalCase` | `LteStack`, `GSMBurst`, `LogLevel` |
| Enum values | `UPPER_CASE` (scoped) | `RAT::LTE`, `LogLevel::ERR` |
| Functions / methods | `camelCase` | `encodePdu()`, `ratToString()` |
| Member variables | `camelCase` (no prefix) | `cellId`, `dlFreqMHz` |
| Constants / `constexpr` | `UPPER_CASE` | `UMTS_CHIP_RATE`, `GSM_SLOTS_PER_FRAME` |
| Type aliases | `PascalCase` | `ByteBuffer`, `CellId`, `RNTI` |
| File names | `snake_case` | `s1ap_codec.cpp`, `lte_stack.h` |

## Headers

- Always use `#pragma once` (no `#ifndef` guards).
- Order: own header, project headers (`../common/types.h`), then STL/system.
- Generated ASN.1 headers (`src/generated/`) must be included inside `extern "C" { }` blocks.
- Forward-declare C structs instead of pulling in generated headers in `.h` files:
  ```cpp
  extern "C" { struct S1AP_PDU; }
  ```

## C++ Style

- Standard: **C++17**. Extensions disabled (`CMAKE_CXX_EXTENSIONS OFF`).
- Prefer `std::thread` for per-RAT execution; do not introduce new threading primitives.
- Use `std::chrono` for all time values; avoid raw `int` milliseconds.
- Use `ByteBuffer` (`std::vector<uint8_t>`) as the canonical byte container.
- Avoid bare `new`/`delete`; use smart pointers or stack allocation.
- Windows: define `WIN32_LEAN_AND_MEAN`, `NOMINMAX`, `NOGDI` before `<windows.h>`.

## Logging

Use the shared `rbs::Logger` from `src/common/logger.h`:
```cpp
LOG_INFO("S1AP", "Setup request sent, eNB-ID=" << enbId);
LOG_DBG ("LTE",  "Tick " << tick);
LOG_ERR ("OMS",  "Config key missing: " << key);
```
Do NOT use `std::cout` / `printf` in module code.

## CMake

- One CMake `STATIC` target per module: `rbs_common`, `rbs_hal`, `rbs_gsm`, `rbs_umts`, `rbs_lte`, `rbs_oms`.
- ASN.1 libraries are separate targets: `rbs_asn1_s1ap`, `rbs_asn1_x2ap` (built in `build-asn1/`).
- New source files must be added to the corresponding `add_library()` block in `CMakeLists.txt`.
- Do NOT globbing (`GLOB`/`GLOB_RECURSE`) for sources.

## ASN.1 Pipeline

- **Never edit** files under `src/generated/` manually — they are regenerated by `gen_asn1.sh`.
- To fix a generated-file issue, update the appropriate `fix_*.py` script:
  | Script | Purpose |
  |--------|---------|
  | `fix_asn1_encoding.py` | Non-ASCII chars in `.asn` spec files |
  | `fix_msvc_asn1.py` | MSVC-specific compile errors in generated C |
  | `fix_protocol_ie_field.py` | Duplicate static symbols in `ProtocolIE-Field.c` |
  | `fix_static_redefs.py` | Other duplicate static definitions |
  | `fix_x2ap_c_includes.py` | Missing/wrong `#include` in X2AP generated files |
  | `fix_x2ap_cycles.py` | Circular include chains in X2AP headers |
  | `fix_x2ap_pdu_alias.py` | PDU type alias mismatches |
- Generation order: `fix_asn1_encoding.py` → `gen_asn1.sh` → fix scripts → CMake build.

## Protocol References

- S1AP: 3GPP TS 36.413
- X2AP: 3GPP TS 36.423
- NBAP: 3GPP TS 25.433
- Cite the spec section in comments when implementing IE handling: `// TS 36.413 §8.7.3`
